results.title: How death came to earth
results.creator_credit: Ishu Patel and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: A legend from India, interpreted on film by a film-maker from India. It is a story of gods and men, of suns and moons and earth, interpreted with an animation style and a richness of colour and design as arresting to the eye as the story and the music are to the ear. Sometimes the illustrations are painted on cells, sometimes the figures are cut-outs moving across shining grounds, but always the pace is gentle, inevitable.

results.title: How clear the hot springs bubble
results.brief_description: Iceland is a country of great contrasts. Under glaciers and close by ice-capped peaks are many examples of thermal activity. In the volcanic belt which stretches across the island there are high temperature zones with bubbling mud pools. These springs are used for swimming pools, hot houses for growing vegetables and flowers and for heating homes.

results.title: How Britain votes
results.brief_description: The General Election of 1950 won by Atlee, is taken as an example to illustrate the functioning of the British electoral system. Footage of the election machinery is shown; the selection of candidate; the work of his local election agent; canvassing the electorate; policing; the work of the returning officer; Polling Day; announcement of the results; State opening of Parliament by George V.

results.title: How blood clots
results.brief_description: Shows what role each part of the blood playsin the clotting process. Follows the actual generation of platelets from large cells found in the bone marrow.

results.title: How atoms combine
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Introducing Chemistry”.
Introduces chemical bonding by explaining the different ways in which atoms combine. Illustrates covalent bonds by showing models of hydrogen atoms, their shell structure and methods of bonding. Employs water molecules as examples of polar covalent bonds and sodium chloride molecules as examples of ionic bonds.

results.brief_description: Animals are equipped in various ways for moving about, according to their environment and needs; wings, fins, the number of legs, and so on are studied.

results.title: Picture Canada
results.creator_credit: Jim Turpie, Crawley Films and Kodak Canada Inc.
results.brief_description: The work of fifteen photographers went into the filming of this memorable picture of Canada - vivid coast to coast impressions of the Canadian mosaic, the diversity of scene, life and culture with historical re-enactments. Fast-paced and entertaining using innovative cinematography and early 70’s music soundtrack.

results.title: How animals defend themselves
results.brief_description: Animals protect themselves from attacks by various means: some depend on their speed and agility, others rely on thick shells or hides, or on sharp claws, teeth, and beaks; others again use camouflage and mimicry. As their environment or their enemies change, so by adaptation do animals’ defence mechanisms evolve.

results.title: How animal life begins
results.brief_description: Portrays the ovulation, fertilization and early development of the mammalian egg. Scenes of spermatizoa attacking the ovum, and time-lapse micro-photographs of cell division in the fertilized egg.
